_note: audit-log.sh is PostToolUse (matcher: *) and append-only: it writes a timestamp\tkind\ttool\tdetail line via somi::audit and never emits a stdout decision. These cases assert the audit-log side-effect (via the runner's per-case SOMI_AUDIT_LOG default, $case_dir/audit.log) rather than additionalContext. Every case was verified against the current, unmodified hooks/post-tool/audit-log.sh by direct invocation before authoring these cases, not assumed. F-14 (phase 2 iteration 2.6): the last two cases pin the `*)` default-tool branch (compact-JSON tool_input, `jq -c ... | head -c 240`), which had no Phase-0 parity anchor. Both were captured by direct invocation against the unmodified bash hook BEFORE hooks/post-tool/audit-log.mjs existed, not hand-computed. NOTE on the boundary case's assertion strength: the runner's `expect_audit_log` is a contains-only substring check, so it structurally cannot detect a port that fails to truncate at all (an untruncated superset would still 'contain' the correctly-truncated prefix) — it only guards against under-truncation and content drift. True truncation-boundary parity (240 vs 241 bytes, byte-exact) was additionally verified via a manual differential probe outside the fixture corpus (see progress.md/diary.md, iteration 2.6) since the runner has no exact-length or exclusion assertion for audit-log content.
